schedules.scheduleName: Schedule S – Details of Income from Salary
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required if taxpayer has salary or pension income during FY 2025-26.
schedules.description: Captures salary income breakup including basic pay, HRA, allowances, perquisites, and employer contributions. Employer-wise details must be entered. Standard deduction of ₹75,000 (new regime) or ₹50,000 (old regime) is claimed here.
keyFields: Gross salary (as per Form 16 Part B)
Profits in lieu of salary u/s 17(3)
crossReferences: Form 16 Part A and Part B
commonMistakes: Not matching salary income with Form 26AS/AIS/TIS
Claiming HRA exemption in new regime (not allowed)
Forgetting to include salary from previous employer in same FY
Not disclosing perquisites – car, ESOP vesting
Incorrect standard deduction amount (₹75,000 new regime vs ₹50,000 old regime)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule HP – Income from House Property
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required if taxpayer owns or is deemed to own any house property (self-occupied, let-out, or deemed let-out).
schedules.description: Captures income or loss from house properties. Self-occupied property interest deduction limited to ₹2 lakh (old regime) or no deduction (new regime). Let-out property: 30% standard deduction on NAV, interest on home loan (no cap for let-out). Municipal taxes paid are deductible.
keyFields: Property address, co-owner details, percentage of ownership
Gross Annual Value (GAV) or Annual Lettable Value
Net Annual Value (NAV = GAV – Municipal Taxes)
Pre-construction interest (1/5th each year for 5 years)
Net income or loss from each property
crossReferences: Schedule CYLA (set-off of house property loss against salary income)
commonMistakes: Claiming interest deduction in new regime (not allowed for self-occupied property)
Declaring only self-occupied property and forgetting deemed let-out for second house
Not entering co-owner's PAN and share percentage
Claiming municipal taxes for which no receipt exists
Not spreading pre-construction period interest over 5 years

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required if taxpayer has any capital gains or capital losses from any asset during FY 2025-26.
schedules.description: Comprehensive schedule capturing short-term and long-term capital gains from all assets: equity shares, mutual funds, real estate, gold, bonds, etc. The schedule is split between transactions before 23 July 2024 (old rates) and on/after 23 July 2024 (new rates per Finance Act 2024).
keyFields: Cost of acquisition (with indexation benefit for LTCG where applicable)
STCG u/s 111A (20%) – listed equity and equity MF
STCG at normal slab rates – other assets
LTCG u/s 112A (12.5% above ₹1.25L) – equity and equity MF
LTCG at 20% – real estate (with indexation), gold, bonds
commonMistakes: Not splitting transactions by 23 July 2024 cut-off date for correct tax rates
Applying indexation on equity shares (not allowed)
Incorrect holding period for LTCG – listed equity: 12 months, real estate: 24 months (from FY 2024-25), gold: 24 months
Forgetting capital gains on redemption of debt mutual funds (taxed at slab rates from April 2023)
Not reporting bonus shares or rights shares cost basis correctly
Missing STT credits on equity transactions

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule 112A – LTCG on Sale of Equity Shares / Units of Equity-Oriented Funds
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Mandatory when LTCG exists on listed equity shares, equity-oriented mutual funds, or units of business trusts (REITs/InvITs) on which STT is paid.
schedules.description: Scrip-by-scrip or fund-by-fund details of long-term capital gains qualifying for tax u/s 112A at 12.5%. Includes grandfathering provisions for assets purchased before 31 January 2018 (Fair Market Value as on 31 Jan 2018 is the deemed cost). For FY 2025-26, the exemption threshold is ₹1.25 lakh.
keyFields: Fair Market Value as on 31 January 2018 (for pre-2018 acquisitions)
crossReferences: Broker contract notes and DEMAT account statement
Schedule TDS2 (TDS on capital gains if any)
commonMistakes: Not using grandfathering (FMV as on 31 Jan 2018) for pre-2018 purchased shares
Entering total portfolio value instead of per-share FMV
Not splitting buys and sells across multiple brokers
Applying ₹1 lakh exemption instead of ₹1.25 lakh (old limit)
Including ELSS redemptions before 3-year lock-in (these are STCG, not LTCG)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule OS – Income from Other Sources
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required if taxpayer has interest income, dividend income, lottery winnings, gifts, or any other residual income.
schedules.description: Captures all income not categorized under Salary, House Property, Business, or Capital Gains. Key items include savings account interest (SBI, post office), FD interest, dividends from domestic and foreign companies, winnings from lottery/games, gifts from non-relatives exceeding ₹50,000, and income from letting of machinery.
keyFields: Interest from fixed deposits and recurring deposits
Winnings from lottery, crossword, card games u/s 115BB (30%)
Family pension (received by legal heirs of deceased employee)
Royalty income if not from profession
Income from letting of plant and machinery
crossReferences: AIS/TIS for verification of dividend and interest income
commonMistakes: Not declaring interest from post office savings accounts (₹3,500 deduction u/s 80TTA available)
Forgetting to include dividend income (taxable since FY 2020-21)
Claiming 80TTA deduction in new regime (not allowed)
Not declaring foreign dividends received in overseas accounts
Including NRE account interest as taxable (NRE interest is exempt for NRIs)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule VI-A – Deductions under Chapter VI-A
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required if taxpayer opts for old tax regime and claims any deductions u/s 80C to 80U.
schedules.description: All deductions available under Chapter VI-A of the Income Tax Act. Under the new regime (default), most deductions are not available except 80CCD(2) (employer NPS contribution), 80CCH (Agniveer corpus), and 80JJAA. Taxpayer must explicitly opt for old regime to claim these deductions.
keyFields: 80C: LIC, PPF, ELSS, tuition fees, home loan principal, NSC (max ₹1.5 lakh)
80CCD(2): Employer NPS contribution (available in new regime also)
80D: Medical insurance premium (self ₹25,000 + parents ₹25,000 or ₹50,000 if senior)
80E: Interest on education loan (no limit, up to 8 years)
80EEA: Interest on home loan for affordable housing (up to ₹1.5 lakh, loan sanctioned by 31 Mar 2022)
80GG: HRA deduction for those not receiving HRA
80TTB: Interest deduction for senior citizens up to ₹50,000
80U: Disability deduction (₹75,000 or ₹1.25 lakh for severe)
crossReferences: Schedule S (salary income for determining regime eligibility)
Form 16 Part B for employer-claimed deductions
commonMistakes: Claiming deductions under new regime (only 80CCD(2) and 80JJAA allowed in new regime)
Exceeding ₹1.5 lakh aggregate limit for 80C + 80CCC + 80CCD(1)
Claiming 80EEA for loans sanctioned after 31 March 2022
Not claiming 80CCD(1B) separately – it is over and above 80C limit
Treating pension received from annuity as not taxable (it is taxable u/s 80CCC withdrawal)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule CFL – Statement of Losses to be Carried Forward
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required if taxpayer has any losses remaining after set-off that are to be carried forward to subsequent assessment years.
schedules.description: Records unadjusted losses from the current year and brought forward losses from earlier years. Capital losses must be specified as STCL or LTCL. Business losses (non-speculative) can be carried forward for 8 years and can only be set off against business income. Loss from house property is set off against any income up to ₹2 lakh, balance carried forward for 8 years.
keyFields: STCL brought forward (can offset STCG and LTCG)
LTCL brought forward (can only offset LTCG)
Current year unabsorbed house property loss
Verification that returns were filed within due date for carry forward
crossReferences: Schedule BFLA (set-off of brought forward losses)
Previous year's ITR acknowledgment and schedule CFL
commonMistakes: Carrying forward losses when original return was filed after due date (loss lapses if return is belated)
Setting off LTCL against STCG at normal slab rates (LTCL can only offset LTCG)
Not maintaining AY-wise segregation of losses
Forgetting speculative business losses (only offset against speculative profits)
Claiming capital loss deduction when STT was not paid on the transaction

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule FA – Details of Foreign Assets and Income from any Source outside India
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Mandatory for Resident and Ordinarily Resident (ROR) individuals holding any foreign asset or receiving income from outside India at any time during FY 2025-26. NRIs and RNOR are exempt from Schedule FA.
schedules.description: Captures details of foreign bank accounts, financial interests, immovable property abroad, capital assets outside India, signing authority in foreign accounts, trusts abroad, and foreign income. Failure to disclose attracts severe penalties under Black Money Act 2015 (₹10 lakh per default plus possible prosecution).
keyFields: Foreign bank accounts: Bank name, SWIFT code, account number, country, peak balance, closing balance
Financial interest in any entity outside India
Immovable property outside India: Address, acquisition date, investment amount
Capital assets outside India: ESOP in foreign company, foreign stocks
Foreign income not derived from business/profession
crossReferences: Schedule TR (tax relief for taxes paid outside India)
FEMA compliance for remittances under LRS
commonMistakes: Not disclosing NRE/NRO accounts (NRIs are exempt but RORs must disclose NRE accounts)
Omitting ESOP shares in foreign company granted by multinational employer
Not reporting foreign accounts even if balance is zero (disclosure required if account existed)
Incorrect conversion of foreign currency balances (use RBI reference rate on 31 March 2026)
Skipping Schedule FA after returning to India thinking NRI exemption applies

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule FSI – Details of Income Accruing or Arising outside India
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required for Resident and Ordinarily Resident (ROR) taxpayers having any income from outside India during FY 2025-26.
schedules.description: Details of income arising in foreign countries including salary earned abroad (for RORs), foreign dividends, interest on foreign bank accounts, rental income from property abroad, and foreign capital gains. DTAA provisions and foreign tax credit are also coordinated through this schedule.
keyFields: Taxpayer identification number in that country
Income head (salary, house property, capital gains, other sources)
Amount of income in foreign currency
Amount of income in INR (at RBI reference rate)
DTAA article under which relief is claimed
commonMistakes: Not converting foreign income at correct exchange rate
Not claiming DTAA relief where applicable (results in double taxation)
Declaring income net of foreign taxes instead of gross amount
Not filing Form 67 (mandatory for claiming foreign tax credit)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule TR – Statement of Tax Relief Claimed under Section 90/90A/91
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required when taxpayer claims relief from double taxation on foreign income under DTAA (Section 90) or unilateral relief (Section 91).
schedules.description: Documents the foreign tax credit claimed by Indian residents on income earned abroad and already taxed in the source country. Must be supported by Form 67 filed before the due date of return. Without Form 67, foreign tax credit is not allowed.
keyFields: Amount of relief claimed (lower of Indian tax or foreign tax on that income)
commonMistakes: Forgetting to file Form 67 before due date of ITR
Claiming credit in excess of Indian tax on that income
Not maintaining foreign tax payment receipts or TRC (Tax Residency Certificate)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule AMT – Computation of Alternate Minimum Tax payable u/s 115JC
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required for individual/HUF taxpayers claiming certain deductions under Chapter VI-A (80-IA, 80-IB, 80-IC, 80-IE, 80P, 10AA) where adjusted total income exceeds ₹20 lakh.
schedules.description: AMT applies to individuals/HUFs who claim profit-linked deductions and whose adjusted income exceeds ₹20 lakh. AMT rate is 18.5% + surcharge + cess on adjusted total income. AMT credit is available for 15 years.
keyFields: Adjusted total income (total income + deductions added back)
AMT credit brought forward from earlier years
commonMistakes: Not computing AMT despite claiming 10AA SEZ deduction
Forgetting AMT credit from earlier years (reduces tax payable)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule SI – Income chargeable to tax at Special Rates
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required when income exists that is taxed at special rates other than normal slab rates.
schedules.description: Summarizes income chargeable at special tax rates: STCG u/s 111A (20%), LTCG u/s 112 (20%), LTCG u/s 112A (12.5%), lottery winnings u/s 115BB (30%), non-resident foreign income u/s 115E, etc.
commonMistakes: Including STCG 111A income in normal slab computation (it must be separately taxed)
Applying wrong rate after 23 July 2024 change (111A: 20%, 112A: 12.5%)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule PTI – Pass Through Income Details from Business Trust or Investment Fund
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Required when taxpayer receives income from REIT, InvIT, or Category I/II AIF as pass-through income.
schedules.description: REITs (Real Estate Investment Trusts) and InvITs (Infrastructure Investment Trusts) distribute income to unit holders which is characterized as dividend, interest, or capital gains and taxed accordingly in the hands of the investor.
keyFields: Name and PAN of business trust/investment fund
Nature of income (interest, dividend, STCG, LTCG)
crossReferences: Schedule CG (for capital gains from REITs)
commonMistakes: Treating all REIT distributions as dividend (must be split by nature)
Not claiming TDS credit on REIT distributions (deducted at source)
